summary: Iteration 3 verified end-to-end. All 45 backend pytest tests PASS (36 from iter1+2 + 9 new for iter3: Xendit webhook flows, Google exchange 401 on fake session, /admin/analytics structure+auth, BSON date storage, scheduler dedupe with native datetimes). MongoDB payments.due_date confirmed as BSON datetime (11/11 records). Frontend: Google login/register buttons present + navigate to auth.emergentagent.com. AuthCallback with fake session redirects to /login (no crash). Admin Overview renders analytics-monthly-chart (SVG) + analytics-service-chart (SVG) + 3 BigMetric cards. Users search filters (12→1 for 'admin'). Payments status filter 'paid' filters (11→1). Subscriptions search accepts input. No regressions.

issues: Recharts logs warning 'width(-1) and height(-1)' on mount before ResponsiveContainer measures parent — cosmetic, chart still renders once layout settles. Consider aspect ratio or explicit min-height on the parent.

action_items: (Optional) Suppress Recharts width/height warnings by giving ResponsiveContainer parent an explicit aspect or min-height.
(Carry-over) SendGrid/Twilio/Xendit still MOCKED — supply keys to enable real notifications and invoice creation.
(Carry-over) _send_reminder_for_payment uses sync SendGrid/Twilio clients inside async — will block event loop under real keys.
critical_code_review_comments: google_exchange sets access_token cookie with samesite=lax + secure=False, and session_token cookie with samesite=none + secure=True — mixed cookie policies from same endpoint. Fine over HTTPS preview but worth aligning both to samesite=none;secure=true in production.
xendit_webhook accepts any token when XENDIT_WEBHOOK_TOKEN env is empty. Intentional for dev per E1, but production deploy MUST set the env var otherwise webhook is open.
Analytics endpoint iterates every paid payment and does 2 DB round-trips per row (subscription + service) — O(N) queries. Fine for <1k payments; consider $lookup aggregation later.
AdminDashboard.jsx is 916 lines — split into per-tab modules for maintainability.

AuthCallback uses window.history.replaceState + navigate — fine, but the raced call to /auth/me while hash still contains session_id could log 401 in console (already gated in AuthContext per E1's note).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iter3.py (new — 9 tests: Xendit webhook happy/ignore/expired paths, Google exchange 401, analytics auth+structure, BSON date storage, scheduler dedupe over BSON)

success_rate.frontend: 100% of iter3 flows (Google buttons, AuthCallback, charts, search bars, status filter)

seed_data_creation: Test suite creates iter3_<ts>@example.com user, netflix subscriptions and multiple payments (some marked paid via Xendit webhook, some for scheduler dedupe test). No cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run backend tests with `pytest -o addopts='' backend/tests/`. Iter3 introduced 9 new tests in test_iter3.py. All 45 tests currently green. BSON date migration in lifespan is idempotent. Google exchange requires real Emergent flow — do NOT try to force it with real Google auth. Xendit webhook token is empty in preview (accepts any) — intentional for dev.
